title: Reset Heartbeat Statistics On The VISU_DATA Screen
summary: Use the Heartbeat RESET button on the VISU_DATA screen to reset heartbeat timing data to zero. The source describes the Heartbeat section as showing Last, Max, and Min values in milliseconds and states that the RESET button resets the data to zero.
when_to_use: Use this procedure when heartbeat timing statistics on the VISU_DATA screen need to be cleared to zero using the HMI RESET control.
not_for: Do not use if the displayed heartbeat values must be preserved as troubleshooting evidence before reset.

roles_and_responsibilities.responsibility: Access the operator station HMI, review the Heartbeat values on the VISU_DATA screen, press RESET, and verify the values return to zero.

safety_notes: Use caution if heartbeat values are needed as troubleshooting evidence before reset.
The source notes that a heartbeat signal longer than 10 seconds can cause operation issues with the tipper due to mis-synchronization.
access_or_tools_needed: Access to the operator station HMI

access_required: Access to the operator station HMI
system_preconditions: VISU_DATA screen is available on the operator station HMI.
Heartbeat section is visible on the VISU_DATA screen.
external_references: Figure 4-22, Operator Station HMI Data Screen—Heartbeat Stats

visual_references.description: Operator Station HMI data screen showing heartbeat signal statistics between the tipper and WCS, including Last, Max, Min, and RESET.
Operator Station HMI VISU_DATA screen layout reference.

steps.title: Open the VISU_DATA screen and locate the Heartbeat section
steps.purpose: Navigate to the HMI area where heartbeat statistics are displayed and can be reset.
steps.instruction: Open the operator station HMI VISU_DATA screen and locate the Heartbeat section that shows heartbeat signal statistics between the tipper and WCS.
steps.expected_result: The Heartbeat section is visible on the VISU_DATA screen.
steps.healthy_condition: The Heartbeat section is present and the heartbeat statistics are readable on the HMI.
steps.failure_condition: The VISU_DATA screen or Heartbeat section cannot be located or viewed.

actions.label: Open VISU_DATA screen
actions.exact_action: Open the VISU_DATA screen and locate the Heartbeat section.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Heartbeat section showing heartbeat signal statistics between the tipper and WCS.
Overall VISU_DATA screen layout to help identify the correct screen.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop or escalate if the VISU_DATA screen cannot be accessed.
Stop or escalate if the Heartbeat section is not visible on the screen.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: The Heartbeat section provides Last, Max, and Min values in milliseconds, and the RESET button resets the data to zero.

steps.title: Identify the RESET button in the Heartbeat section
steps.purpose: Confirm the correct HMI control that performs the heartbeat statistics reset.
steps.instruction: Within the Heartbeat section, identify the RESET button associated with the displayed heartbeat statistics.
steps.expected_result: The RESET button is identified in the Heartbeat section.
steps.healthy_condition: A clearly labeled RESET button is visible in the Heartbeat section.
steps.failure_condition: The RESET button is not visible or cannot be distinguished from other controls.

actions.label: Locate RESET control
actions.exact_action: Identify the RESET button within the Heartbeat section.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Heartbeat section showing the RESET button.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop or escalate if the RESET button is not present in the Heartbeat section.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: The Heartbeat section provides Last, Max, and Min values in milliseconds, and the RESET button resets the data to zero.

steps.title: Review the displayed heartbeat values
steps.purpose: Confirm which values are currently displayed before clearing them.
steps.instruction: Review the displayed Last, Max, and Min heartbeat values in milliseconds before performing the reset.
steps.expected_result: The current Last, Max, and Min heartbeat values are reviewed before reset.
steps.healthy_condition: Last, Max, and Min values are visible and readable in the Heartbeat section.
steps.failure_condition: The heartbeat values are missing, unreadable, or cannot be confirmed before reset.

actions.label: Review heartbeat values
actions.exact_action: Review the displayed Last, Max, and Min heartbeat values that will be reset.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Last, Max, and Min heartbeat values in milliseconds.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop if the values need to be preserved as troubleshooting evidence before reset.
Escalate if the heartbeat values cannot be read on the HMI.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: The Heartbeat section provides Last, Max, and Min values in milliseconds, and the RESET button resets the data to zero.

steps.title: Press RESET to clear the heartbeat statistics
steps.purpose: Reset the heartbeat timing data to zero using the documented HMI control.
steps.instruction: Press the RESET button in the Heartbeat section to reset the displayed heartbeat data to zero.
steps.expected_result: The heartbeat statistics are reset by the HMI.
steps.healthy_condition: The HMI accepts the RESET action and the heartbeat statistics clear to zero.
steps.failure_condition: Pressing RESET does not clear the heartbeat statistics to zero.

actions.label: Press RESET
actions.exact_action: Press the RESET button to reset the heartbeat data to zero.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Heartbeat section and RESET button used to clear the data.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if the RESET button does not reset the displayed Heartbeat values to zero.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: The Heartbeat section provides Last, Max, and Min values in milliseconds, and the RESET button resets the data to zero.

steps.title: Verify the heartbeat values display zero
steps.instruction: Verify that the Heartbeat values on the VISU_DATA screen display zero after the reset.
steps.healthy_condition: Last, Max, and Min heartbeat values display zero after RESET.
steps.failure_condition: One or more heartbeat values remain non-zero after RESET.

actions.label: Confirm zero values
actions.exact_action: Verify that the Heartbeat values display zero after the reset.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Heartbeat values after RESET to confirm they display zero.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if the displayed Heartbeat values do not return to zero after RESET.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: The Heartbeat section provides Last, Max, and Min values in milliseconds, and the RESET button resets the data to zero.

success_criteria: The Heartbeat statistics on the VISU_DATA screen are reset to zero.
Last, Max, and Min heartbeat values display zero after pressing RESET.
healthy_conditions: Heartbeat section is visible on the VISU_DATA screen.
Last, Max, and Min values are displayed in milliseconds.
After RESET, the displayed heartbeat values are zero.
failure_conditions: The VISU_DATA screen or Heartbeat section cannot be accessed.
The RESET button is not visible in the Heartbeat section.
The RESET button does not reset the displayed Heartbeat values to zero.
Heartbeat values remain non-zero after reset.
escalation_guidance: Escalate if the RESET button does not reset the displayed Heartbeat values to zero.
Escalate if the Heartbeat section or RESET control is not available on the VISU_DATA screen.
Preserve the displayed values before reset when they may be needed as troubleshooting evidence.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Heartbeat section with Last, Max, Min, and RESET.

source_refs.quote_or_summary: The Heartbeat section provides Last, Max, and Min values in milliseconds, and the RESET button resets the data to zero.

missing_details: The source packet does not provide a documented navigation path to open the VISU_DATA screen.
The source packet does not specify whether production must be stopped before performing this reset.
The source packet does not specify whether LOTO is required.
The source packet does not provide an estimated completion time.
The source packet does not define any additional role boundary beyond the candidate assignment to L1_support.
